---
title: "fMRI Windowing"
subtitle: "Compatibility queries for stimulus features on run clocks."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

fMRI windowing helpers describe run timing separately from feature timing. A
`RunGrid` stores the TR, run start, run length, named clocks, and an explicit
feature-to-experiment `ClockMap`. Query helpers slice feature objects by run and
optionally resample to the run TR grid.

These are compatibility helpers. The primary `natural_features` contract ends
at lossless native-time bookkeeping and `TemporalPayload`; new HRF/TR/design
workflows should live in downstream modeling libraries.

## Invariants

- `tr_s` must be positive.
- `n_trs_by_run` must contain positive integers.
- `run_starts_s`, when supplied, must match the number of runs.
- `feature_to_experiment_by_run`, when supplied, must match the number of runs
  and each map must use the declared feature and experiment clocks.
- Legacy `feature_t0_by_run`, when supplied, must match the number of runs.
- Query windows require `t_end_s > t_start_s`.
- Output time can be `absolute`, `run_relative`, or `feature`.

## What it refuses

- Unknown run indices.
- Empty or non-positive run definitions.
- Invalid output-time modes.
- Windows whose end is not later than their start.

## What it doesn't do

Windowing does not estimate an HRF model. TR resampling here remains for
compatibility; prefer handing native payloads to downstream modeling code.

## See also

[Native temporal contract](../spec/native-temporal-contract.qmd) · [Query a run-aware fMRI window](../cookbook/fmri-window-query.qmd) · [fMRI querying reference](../reference/query_feature_window_tr.qmd)
